feature: izba desktop app — Manifest tab (izba.yml diff/promote/export)

journeys.rationale: A user who already has a valid izba.yml in their workspace and creates the sandbox from it should see the Manifest tab confirm there is nothing to reconcile. This is the tab's simplest, most common state and the swarm's cheapest way to exercise the manifest_truth oracle's happy path.

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-sync-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-sync-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-sync-demo sandbox's detail view, click its Manifest tab, and click Refresh
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'In sync — izba.yml and managed settings match.'

journeys.rationale: The headline manifest workflow: someone edits izba.yml (here simulating that edit as a precondition of the step, since the GUI Actor has no file-editing surface of its own), the app must show the drift, and Promote must apply it. This is the harness's ground-truth-verified decisive assertion — manifest_diff's reported state must match `izba diff`'s. The GUI creates the sandbox STOPPED and the seeded drift is an image change (alpine:3.20 -> alpine:3.21): the core promote gate (crates/izba-core/src/manifest/promote.rs:254) refuses an image-class change without restart authorization REGARDLESS of run state, so ManifestTab now shows a stopped-case checkbox (`RESTART_CHECKBOX_LABEL_STOPPED_IMAGE`) that must be ticked before Promote is even clickable. Before that fix (dogfood acceptance run 2, PF1) this journey's final step was a dead end: the dialog had no control that could satisfy the gate and Promote always failed underneath a contradictory 'applies on the next start' note.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.2 Promote in the app; ManifestTab.tsx BANNER_TEXT.repo_ahead + Promote dialog

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-drift-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-drift-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-drift-demo sandbox's detail view, click its Manifest tab, and click Refresh to see whether izba.yml has any changes not yet applied
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'izba.yml has changes not yet applied. Review below, then Promote.' with a table row showing the image changing from alpine:3.20 to alpine:3.21

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.21

steps.intent: click 'Promote…' on the Manifest tab, tick the 'Start the sandbox to apply the image change (the scratch disk is kept, not reset)' checkbox, then confirm by clicking 'Promote' in the dialog that appears
steps.expect: the dialog reports 'Promoted 1 change(s).' and 'Sandbox was started to apply the change.', and, after closing it and refreshing, the Manifest tab shows 'In sync — izba.yml and managed settings match.'

journeys.rationale: A workspace with no izba.yml at all is the common first-time state (nothing was ever exported); the tab must guide the user to create one rather than show a bare error or an empty table. NOT marked core: real-bridge.js records a manifest_diff invoke-log digest only on a successful (ok) response (app/dogfood/real-bridge.js __dfHandleMessage), and ManifestTab's missing-manifest path is api.manifestDiff() REJECTING (its .catch sets `error`, never `diff`). commands.rs's `manifest_diff_core` checks `workspace.join("izba.yml").exists()` BEFORE calling `compute_diff` and, when it's genuinely absent, returns the exact string `no izba.yml found in workspace` — a stable sentinel distinct from a parse failure (`compute_diff` -> `Manifest::load_str` -> `.context("parsing izba.yml")`), which also mentions izba.yml but is a different, honest-to-render error. ManifestTab keys its guidance panel on that sentinel (`missingManifest = error !== null && error.includes("no izba.yml found")`), so a corrupt izba.yml now falls through to the raw error area instead of being mislabeled as absent. So this step's manifest_diff call never carries a `digest`, manifest_truth_oracle's `manifest_diff_seen` check stays false, and a `core: true` here would flip run_gui_journeys.py's `unreached_decisive` path even though the Actor did exactly what the step asked — a false product-negative, not a real one. The dom_expect oracle (which does not require a digest) still verifies this step from the rendered banner text.

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field and 'manifest-missing-demo' into the Name field (leave Image at its default), then click Create to create a new sandbox from this empty workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-missing-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-missing-demo sandbox's detail view and click its Manifest tab
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows "No izba.yml found in this sandbox's workspace." along with guidance to run 'izba export' or use Export to create one

journeys.rationale: The bootstrap chicken-and-egg the empty state itself advertises: a workspace with NO izba.yml shows the missing-manifest guidance ('... use Export here after making changes in the app'), so Export must be clickable there and must CREATE the file. Found by the owner post-campaign on a pre-existing sandbox (D4 flag): ManifestTab's canExport gated solely on a non-null diff state (managed_ahead/diverged), but with no izba.yml manifest_diff errors and diff stays null, so 'Export to izba.yml' was permanently disabled in exactly the state whose guidance says to use it — while the backend (izba_core::manifest::ops::export) never reads an existing izba.yml (it writes managed truth, advances base, clears review) and always supported bootstrap export. core sits on the Export click: doExport's automatic post-export diff refetch is the digest-carrying manifest_diff the manifest_truth oracle grades against `izba diff` ground truth, which must now be in_sync — verifying the file was genuinely written, not just that a banner rendered. No expect_text/expect_state hooks: manifest_truth takes precedence for a journey that drives manifest_diff, matching this corpus's other manifest journeys.

source.ref: PR #145 dogfood-fixes/gui-handover (owner-reported post-campaign, D4): ManifestTab.tsx canExport missing-manifest bootstrap fix + MISSING_MANIFEST_BODY 'use Export here' + doExport refetch; crates/izba-core/src/manifest/ops.rs export

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field and 'manifest-bootstrap-demo' into the Name field (leave Image at its default), then click Create to create a new sandbox from this empty workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-bootstrap-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-bootstrap-demo sandbox's detail view and click its Manifest tab
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows "No izba.yml found in this sandbox's workspace." along with guidance to run 'izba export' or use Export to create one, and the 'Export to izba.yml' button is enabled

steps.intent: click 'Export to izba.yml' on the Manifest tab to create the file from the sandbox's managed settings, then observe the tab
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'Exported to {workspace}/izba.yml' and the banner now reads 'In sync — izba.yml and managed settings match.' — the export bootstrapped the missing izba.yml and the tab's own diff refetch confirms it matches managed truth

journeys.rationale: The other half of the reconciliation story (complementing manifest-seeded-drift-promote's repo_ahead/Promote path): a user changes a sandbox's *live* settings through the app itself — here, editing the egress policy on the Policy tab (PolicyEditor.tsx) and clicking its Save button, which calls `policy_set_full` and mutates the managed truth (policy.yaml) without touching izba.yml — then opens the Manifest tab and must see the managed_ahead banner ('Live settings have drifted from izba.yml. Export to capture them.'), and Export must write that drift back into the file AND leave the tab showing the resulting in-sync state, not a stale managed_ahead re-render. `core: true` sits on the EXPORT step (moved here from the managed_ahead-view step, run-3 skeptic H3): a run-3 acceptance swarm reached the managed_ahead banner honestly (real `policy_set_full` -> real `manifest_diff` managed_ahead, ground-truth matched) but the journey ended there without ever clicking Export, so the flagship's actual round trip — Export writing the drift back into izba.yml and the tab settling on in-sync — went completely unverified while still reading as a clean pass. `ManifestTab.tsx`'s `doExport` re-calls `load()` after a successful export (mirroring `confirmPromote`, which already refetches after promote), so the Export click's own `manifest_diff` call is itself the decisive invoke this oracle grades: `run_gui_journeys.py`'s manifest_truth oracle always grades the LAST manifest_diff digest against `izba diff` ground truth, so putting `core: true` here forces the harness to treat the journey as incomplete (`unreached_decisive`) unless the Actor actually clicks Export and the post-export digest is the one ground-truthed — verifying the full write-back, not just the drift detection. The preceding Policy-tab save is NOT marked core — it never invokes manifest_diff, so marking it core would wrongly gate decisiveness on a step the oracle cannot verify; the managed_ahead-view step's own outcome is still checked by the ordinary end-of-journey dom_expect oracle against its banner text. This journey is also the one that ground-truths two GUI-manifest fixes: (1) the create-time manifest-base-seeding fix — `create_core` (app/src-tauri/src/commands.rs) mirrors `izba create`'s post-Create seeding (`write_manifest_base` calling the same `store::write_base`/`clear_review` + `EgressPolicyConfig::write_to` the CLI's `persist_policy_config` uses) for a sandbox created from a seeded workspace. Before that fix, `compute_diff`'s `base = managed.clone()` fallback (no `manifest.base.yaml` on disk for a GUI-created sandbox) made managed_ahead structurally unreachable here: the Policy-tab edit would have been misclassified as repo_ahead against the unchanged izba.yml, and this journey's decisive step would have asserted a banner the app could never show for a GUI-created sandbox. (2) the export-refetch fix — see above; before it, the Export click's `manifest_diff` call would still have carried the stale pre-export managed_ahead digest, and this decisive step could never observe an in-sync ground truth at all.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.1 Manifest tab; ManifestTab.tsx BANNER_TEXT.managed_ahead/in_sync + doExport; PolicyEditor.tsx save(); app/src-tauri/src/commands.rs write_manifest_base; dogfood-app-guide.md Policy / egress firewall + Manifest (izba.yml)

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-export-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-export-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-export-demo sandbox's detail view, click its Policy tab, click 'Add host', type 'example.com' into the new row's Host field, then click Save
steps.expect: the Policy tab shows 'saved · reloaded'

steps.intent: click the Manifest tab, then click Refresh to see whether the live settings have drifted from izba.yml
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'Live settings have drifted from izba.yml. Export to capture them.'

steps.intent: click 'Export to izba.yml' on the Manifest tab
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'Exported to {workspace}/izba.yml', confirming the live policy change was captured back into the file, and the banner now reads 'In sync — izba.yml and managed settings match.' since the Export click's own diff refetch is now in sync

journeys.rationale: A promote that loosens the egress firewall must not sail through silently: izba.yml flips egress enforcement from on to off (a weakening the diff engine flags weakens_egress), the delta table must carry the red '⚠ weakens egress' marker, and the Promote confirm dialog must gate the Promote button behind an explicit acknowledgment checkbox before it will apply. egress is a live-class field, so no image-restart checkbox is in play — only the weakens-ack gate. core marks the repo_ahead diff view that renders the weakens marker; the ack+promote is dom_expect-graded and leaves the tab in-sync (post-journey ground truth), so the truth oracle's last digest matches.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.2 (weakens-egress acknowledgment) + §6; ManifestTab.tsx WEAKENS_ACK_LABEL + '⚠ weakens egress'; crates/izba-core/src/manifest/diff.rs egress_weakens; dogfood-app-guide.md 'weakens egress'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20
  egress:
    enforce: true
    allow:
      - example.com

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-weakens-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-weakens-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-weakens-demo sandbox's detail view, click its Manifest tab, and click Refresh to review izba.yml's pending changes
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'izba.yml has changes not yet applied. Review below, then Promote.' and the changed egress row carries a red '⚠ weakens egress' marker

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20
  egress:
    enforce: false
    allow:
      - example.com

steps.intent: click 'Promote…', try to confirm, notice the Promote button is not armed until you tick 'I understand this weakens the egress firewall', then tick it and confirm
steps.expect: the confirm dialog requires ticking 'I understand this weakens the egress firewall' before Promote is enabled, and after confirming it reports 'Promoted 1 change(s).'

journeys.rationale: A live-class change (adding a published port) promoted onto a RUNNING sandbox must apply immediately with NO next-start caveat — distinguishing it from restart/image-class promotes. Run-3 revision: the GUI creates the sandbox STOPPED, so the journey now explicitly STARTS the sandbox and confirms it is running BEFORE seeding the drift and promoting — otherwise the 'live on a running sandbox' promise is never actually exercised (the run-2 miss). The port delta must be tagged 'live' and the promote outcome must apply immediately with no restart/stopped note. core marks the repo_ahead diff view; the promote is dom_expect-graded and refetches to in-sync.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.1 delta class chip + §6 promote happy path (live-class field, no restart); ManifestTab.tsx CLASS_TOOLTIP.live + showRestartNote/showRestartCheckbox; crates/izba-core/src/manifest/diff.rs ports->FieldClass::Live; dogfood-app-guide.md 'Start' / 'Manifest (izba.yml)'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-live-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-live-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-live-demo sandbox's detail view and, on its Overview, click Start to boot the sandbox; wait until its status shows running
steps.expect: the manifest-live-demo sandbox is running

steps.intent: click the Manifest tab and click Refresh to review the pending izba.yml change
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'izba.yml has changes not yet applied. Review below, then Promote.' and the changed ports row is tagged 'live'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20
  ports:
    - guest: 8080
      host: 8080

steps.intent: click 'Promote…' and confirm the change in the dialog
steps.expect: the dialog reports 'Promoted 1 change(s).' applied immediately, with no 'apply on next start' or 'Sandbox is stopped' caveat, and the banner returns to 'In sync — izba.yml and managed settings match.'

journeys.rationale: Promoting a change to a STOPPED sandbox must tell the user it takes effect on the next start rather than pretending it applied live. Run-3 revision: the GUI already creates the sandbox STOPPED, so the earlier explicit Stop step is dropped (a stopped sandbox shows a Start control, not Stop — the old step was unreachable). A resources (cpus) change is restart-class but NOT image-class, so no promote-gate checkbox blocks it; the outcome must carry the 'Sandbox is stopped — changes apply on next start' note. core marks the repo_ahead diff view; the promote is dom_expect-graded and refetches to in-sync.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.2 confirm dialog restart implications + §6 promote on stopped sandbox; ManifestTab.tsx 'Sandbox is stopped — changes apply on next start.' + RESTART_NOTE; crates/izba-core/src/manifest/diff.rs cpus->FieldClass::Restart

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-stopped-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-stopped-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-stopped-demo sandbox's detail view, click the Manifest tab, and click Refresh to review the pending izba.yml change
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'izba.yml has changes not yet applied. Review below, then Promote.' with a row changing cpus from 2 to 4

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20
  resources:
    cpus: 4

steps.intent: click 'Promote…' and confirm the change in the dialog
steps.expect: the dialog reports 'Promoted 1 change(s).' and notes 'Sandbox is stopped — changes apply on next start.'

journeys.rationale: When BOTH sides change — the user edits managed truth via the Policy tab AND izba.yml is edited independently — the tab must render the diverged banner that explains Promote applies izba.yml while Export overwrites it, rather than mislabeling one side. Cheaply reachable by combining a policy-edit (managed drift) with a seeded izba.yml image change (repo drift). core marks the diverged diff view; the journey ends there so post-journey ground truth stays diverged and matches the last UI digest.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.1 diverged state + §6 drift rendering per state; ManifestTab.tsx BANNER_TEXT.diverged; dogfood-app-guide.md 'Diverged'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-diverged-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-diverged-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-diverged-demo sandbox's detail view, click its Policy tab, click 'Add host', type 'example.com' into the new row's Host field, then click Save
steps.expect: the Policy tab shows 'saved · reloaded'

steps.intent: click the Manifest tab and click Refresh to compare izba.yml with the live settings
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'Both izba.yml and managed settings changed. Promote applies izba.yml; Export overwrites it.'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.21

journeys.rationale: The review-token TOCTOU guard: after the user reviews a diff, if izba.yml changes again before they Promote (without re-reviewing), Promote must refuse and tell them to Refresh and review again — no silent apply of unreviewed bytes. Run-3 revision: the two seeded edits are now ports (live-class) changes, NOT image changes — an image delta would trip the new client-side image-restart gate (`requiresRestartForImage` disables Promote until the stopped-image checkbox is ticked), which would prevent the click from ever reaching the backend token check. A ports change is live-class, so Promote is directly clickable and the backend stale-token refusal is what surfaces. NOT marked core: the decisive assertion is a refusal error surfaced in the promote dialog (dom_expect), not a fresh manifest_diff digest; both seeded edits are single ports-live deltas so any digest comparison still agrees (repo_ahead / 1 delta / no weakens).

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.2 review-token parity / stale-token refusal + §6; ManifestTab.tsx STALE_TOKEN_ERROR + mapPromoteError; dogfood-app-guide.md 'Refresh re-reads both sides'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20

steps.intent: type {workspace} into the Workspace field, 'manifest-stale-demo' into the Name field, and 'alpine:3.20' into the Image field, then click Create to create a new sandbox from this workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox named manifest-stale-demo appears in the sandbox list

steps.intent: open the manifest-stale-demo sandbox's detail view, click its Manifest tab, and click Refresh to review the pending izba.yml change
steps.expect: the Manifest tab shows 'izba.yml has changes not yet applied. Review below, then Promote.' with a published-port change (host 8080)

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20
  ports:
    - guest: 8080
      host: 8080

steps.intent: without clicking Refresh again, click 'Promote…' and confirm Promote in the dialog (izba.yml has since changed on disk once more)
steps.expect: Promote is refused with the message 'izba.yml changed since you viewed this diff. Refresh and review again.'

seed_files.izba.yml:
apiVersion: izba.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Sandbox
spec:
  image: alpine:3.20
  ports:
    - guest: 9090
      host: 9090

journeys.rationale: The P1 from the first GUI run: a disabled Create button with no explanation. With required fields empty the dialog must name exactly what's missing beneath the button instead of leaving Create inertly greyed out. No sandbox is created and no manifest_diff runs, so this is core-less and dom_expect-graded on the inline hint copy.

source.ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-gui-manifest-dogfood-design.md §3.4 NewSandbox feedback fix + §6 NewSandbox validation feedback; NewSandbox.tsx createBlockers ('Name is required.' / 'Workspace folder is required.'); dogfood-app-guide.md 'If Create stays disabled, look just below the button'

steps.intent: open the New sandbox dialog, clear the Name field, and leave the Workspace folder empty, then look at the Create button
steps.expect: Create is disabled and inline hints beneath it read 'Name is required.' and 'Workspace folder is required.', naming exactly what must be filled in
